pi_items.verified_value: (4/π) * Sum(sin((2k-1)x)/(2k-1), k, 1, n) for square wave with period 2π
pi_items.source: Wolfram MathWorld (https://mathworld.wolfram.com/FourierSeriesSquareWave.html), Wikipedia, Swarthmore college Fourier materials
Standard Fourier series representation confirmed by Wolfram MathWorld and multiple educational sources
The leading coefficient (4/π) ≈ 1.273 corresponds to unit amplitude square wave. Verified in Fourier series documentation.

privileged_info_summary: This task asks users to create a GeoGebra applet demonstrating Fourier series convergence to a square wave. The mathematical foundation is sound: the formula (4/π) * Sum(sin((2k-1)x)/(2k-1), k, 1, n) is the correct Fourier series representation of a square wave with period 2π and amplitude 1. The required GeoGebra commands (Sum and Slider) are confirmed official commands with full documentation. The file should be saved as 'fourier_square_wave.ggb' in /home/ga/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/. The task uses synthetic data (user-created construction), and the verifier correctly checks for the presence of key elements: file creation during task, slider presence, Sum command usage, square wave definition, and text annotation. The gate mechanism properly enforces that the Sum command (essential for Fourier series) must be present.
